members.name: beginFrame
members.description: Get a list of begin values in frames. 0-based.

members.name: beginSample
members.description: Get a list of begin values in samples. 0-based.

members.name: beginSeconds
members.description: Get a list of begin values in seconds.

members.name: cumulativeFrames
members.description: Get the cumulative time expressed in frames. See `.cumulativeSeconds`.

members.name: cumulativeSample
members.description: Get the cumulative time expressed in samples. See `.cumulativeSeconds`.

members.name: cumulativeSamples
members.description: Get the cumulative time expressed in samples. See `.cumulativeSeconds`.

members.name: cumulativeSeconds
members.description: Get the cumulative time expressed in seconds. It counts from 0 when you Start. Unlike `.runningSeconds`, it is slowed/sped by the Speed parameter, and paused by the Play parameter. It continues to increase if there is any looping, jumping or scrubbing around.

members.name: cumulativeTimecode
members.description: Get the cumulative time as a Timecode Object. See Timecode Class. See `.cumulativeSeconds`.

members.name: masterFrames
members.description: Get or set the master time expressed in frames. 0-based. See `.masterSeconds`.

members.name: masterFrame
members.description: Get or set the master time expressed in frames. 0-based. See `.masterSeconds`.

members.name: masterSamples
members.description: Get or set the master time expressed in samples. See `.masterSeconds`.

members.name: masterSample
members.description: Get or set the master time expressed in samples. See `.masterSeconds`.

members.name: masterSeconds
members.description: Get or set the master time expressed in seconds. It counts from 0 when you Start, `.masterSeconds` is slowed/sped by the Speed parameter, and paused by the Play parameter. It jumps to the appropriate time when you scrub. This is the main clock in the Timer CHOP and can be set directly using python (`OP.masterSeconds = val`), or use the `.goTo()` function which has more options. When multi-segments are specified to the Timer CHOP, it reflects the time as if you ran through the segments without interrupting it. If in any segment Cycle is on and Cycle Limit is off, it calculates as if the cycle runs only once.

members.name: masterFraction
members.description: Get or set the master time expressed in fractional form. See `.masterSeconds`.

members.name: masterTimecode
members.description: Get or set the master time expressed as a Timecode Object. See Timecode Class. See `.masterSeconds`.

members.name: cycle
members.description: Get or set the cycle index of the current segment.

members.name: fraction
members.description: Get the time index in fractional form, same as the `timer_fraction` channel. Used in the callbacks, it's more up-to-date to the current frame. (When using segments, it's the first segment).

members.name: playingFrames
members.description: Get the playing time expressed in frames. 0-based. See `.playingSeconds`.

members.name: playingSample
members.description: Get the playing time expressed in samples. See `.playingSeconds`.

members.name: playingSamples
members.description: Get the playing time expressed in samples. See `.playingSeconds`.

members.name: playingSeconds
members.description: Get the playing time expressed in seconds. It counts from 0 when you Start. it is unaffected by the Speed parameter, but unlike `.runningSeconds`, it is paused by the Play parameter. It continues to increase if there is any looping, jumping or scrubbing around.

members.name: playingTimecode
members.description: Get the playing time as a Timecode Object. See Timecode Class. See `.playingSeconds`.

members.name: runningFraction
members.description: Get the running time index expressed in fractional form. See `.runningSeconds`. This will be an estimate as the actual length is approximated on start.

members.name: runningFrames
members.description: Get the running time expressed in frames. 0-based. See `.runningSeconds`.

members.name: runningFrame
members.description: Get the running time expressed in frames. 0-based. See `.runningSeconds`.

members.name: runningSamples
members.description: Get the running time index expressed in samples. See `.runningSeconds`.

members.name: runningSample
members.description: Get the running time index expressed in samples. See `.runningSeconds`.

members.name: runningSeconds
members.description: Get the running time expressed in seconds. It keeps counting up after Start and is not affected by changing the Speed or pausing Play or scrubbing. It is basically the "wall clock" after pressing Start. (You normally don't set the value, use `.masterSeconds`.) It doesn't reset to 0 until you Initialize or Start again.

members.name: runningTimecode
members.description: Get the running time index as a Timecode Object. See Timecode Class. See `.runningSeconds`.

members.name: runningLengthFrames
members.description: Get the running length expressed in frames.

members.name: runningLengthSamples
members.description: Get the running length expressed in samples.

members.name: runningLengthSeconds
members.description: Get the running length expressed in seconds.

members.name: runningLengthTimecode
members.description: Get the running length as a Timecode Object. See Timecode Class.

members.name: segment
members.description: Get or set the segment index.

members.name: segments
members.description: Get the list of segments.

members.name: timecode
members.description: Get the master timecode. See Timecode Class.

methods.name: goToNextSegment
methods.description: Jump to the next segment. Equivalent to pulsing the Go to Next Segment parameter on the Segments Page.

methods.name: goToCycleEnd
methods.description: Jump to the end of the current cycle. Equivalent to pulsing the Go to End of Cycle parameter on the Timer Page.

methods.name: goTo
methods.signature: goTogoTo(segment=0, cycle=0, endOfCycle=True, seconds=0, frame=0, sample=0,  fraction=0)
methods.description: Allows the user to jump to a different time index based on the arguments passed in. Only one unit of time (seconds, frame, sample, fraction) can be specified when calling this method. For example, including both seconds and frame will yield an error. If there are multiple segments, and only a unit of time is specified, the method will jump to the corresponding running time index. If either a segment index or a cycle index or both are specified along with a unit of time, the method will jump to the corresponding local time index. There are fifteen different combinations of arguments that the user can pass in. An example of how this method can be called is: timerop.goTo(segment=1, cycle=2, seconds=5, endOfCycle=True). *segment - (Keyword, Optional) If specified, will jump to the indicated segment number (0 is first). *cycle - (Keyword, Optional) If specified, will jump to the indicated cycle number (0 is first). *endOfCycle - (Keyword, Optional) False by default. If specified as True, the goTo() function with the specified arguments will be called again at the end of the cycle, causing a jump only at that time to the specified location. *seconds - (Keyword, Optional) If specified, will jump to the indicated time index. Cannot also specify frame, sample, or fraction. *frame - (Keyword, Optional) If specified, will jump to the indicated time index. Cannot also specify seconds, sample, or fraction. *sample - (Keyword, Optional) If specified, will jump to the indicated time index. Cannot also specify seconds, frame, or fraction. *fraction - (Keyword, Optional) If specified, will jump to the indicated time index. Cannot also specify seconds, frame, or sample.

methods.name: goToPrevSegment
methods.description: Jump to the previous segment. Equivalent to pulsing the Go to Previous Segment parameter on the Segments Page.

methods.name: lastCycle
methods.description: Sets the current cycle to be the last cycle of the current segment. Equivalent to pulsing the Exit at End of Cycle parameter on the Timer Page.

_provenance.fact: scraped from the TimerCHOP Class wiki page
